changes.description: Re-engineered the candidate enrollment process to use opaque invitation codes and admin-triggered notifications. Raw JWTs are no longer returned in enrollment responses or exposed in URLs. Candidates now redeem an opaque code via a secure endpoint to receive their session token in the response body.

migration.before: Raw JWTs returned immediately upon enrollment and exposed in URLs
migration.after: Opaque codes issued; JWTs returned only via POST /access/redeem

changes.description: Implemented branded HTML email notifications for candidate invitations, delivered asynchronously via Kafka when triggered by an administrator.

changes.summary: Removal of face reference URL from candidate profiles
changes.description: The face_reference_url field has been removed from the candidate_profiles table. Face ID management is now handled exclusively during active exam sessions and is no longer stored at the profile level. This change affects candidate creation, updates, and bulk CSV uploads (5th column removed).

migration.before: Storing face reference URL on candidate profiles
migration.after: Face ID managed during exam sessions only

changes.description: Exam randomization rules have been completely removed from the system. All questions must now be manually mapped to exams using the Questions Assembly endpoints. The 'RandomizationRules' field has also been removed from the Exam domain model.

migration.before: Randomization rules logic for dynamic question selection

changes.description: Implemented event-driven notifications for exam lifecycle events (Created, Scheduled, Published, Closed). The service now automatically aggregates recipient data from candidate and enterprise services to support multi-stakeholder alerts.

The user update endpoint now allows self-service updates for all authenticated enterprise users. Enterprise staff can update their own profile fields (e.g., name, phone, honorific), but cannot change their roles. Enterprise administrators retain full access to update any user and manage roles.
Enterprise administrators can now reactivate previously deactivated user accounts. This restores the user's login access and records an audit event.

Implemented a secure, token-based password reset flow. Users can request a reset link via email, which is delivered asynchronously through the notification service. The system uses SHA-256 hashing for tokens and enforces a 30-minute expiry. All responses are non-enumerating for security.

changes.summary: Password reset email template and delivery
changes.description: The notification service now consumes `enterprise.password.reset.requested` events to deliver branded HTML password reset emails to users. The email includes a secure link and expiration notice.
Implemented automated email notifications for enterprise lifecycle events including approval, suspension (administrative/payment), and soft-deletion/retention period starts.

Implemented security and lifecycle notifications for enterprise users, including account activation/deactivation, password change confirmations, and administrative password resets with temporary credential delivery.

changes.summary: Secure enterprise ID extraction for payment history
changes.description: The GET /payments/history endpoint no longer accepts enterpriseId as a query parameter. The enterprise ID is now automatically extracted from the authenticated user's JWT claims (passed via X-Enterprise-ID header by the gateway). This prevents unauthorized access to other enterprises' payment data.

changes.summary: Admin trial provisioning and refund endpoints added
changes.description: System administrators can now provision free trial subscriptions for enterprises and issue refunds for existing invoices.
Enterprise administrators and system administrators can now retrieve detailed information for a single specific payment using its ID.

changes.summary: Added notes field to Payment model
changes.description: A new `notes` field has been added to the Payment model to safely store administrative comments and refund reasons, separating them from provider-specific error messages.

changes.summary: Internal feature gate usage endpoint added
changes.description: An internal endpoint has been added to retrieve plan features for an enterprise's active subscription. This is strictly for internal microservice communication and is not proxied via the API Gateway.

changes.summary: Admin subscription plan management endpoints added
changes.description: System administrators can now fully manage subscription plans directly through the API, including creating, updating, listing, and safely deactivating plans.

changes.summary: Billing summary and invoice endpoints added
changes.description: Enterprise administrators can now retrieve a summarized dashboard of their billing aggregates (YTD paid, outstanding balance, last payment) and list all invoices.

changes.summary: Standardized pagination and dynamic sorting across list endpoints
changes.description: All list endpoints in the payment service now return a standardized `pagination.PaginatedResponse` structure. They support `page` and `limit` query parameters, as well as safe, dynamic sorting via `sort` and `sort_dir` parameters with strict field allow-lists.

All subscription management endpoints have been removed from the enterprise-service. The enterprise-service no longer owns subscription state. Migrate all calls to the equivalent endpoints on the payment-service.

migration.after: Automated via Kafka event — no client call needed

changes.description: The payment-service is now the single source of truth for all subscription state and Stripe interactions. Four self-service and one admin endpoint are available.
System administrators can now manually set subscription state (plan, status, dates) without triggering a Stripe operation. Useful for trials and manual adjustments.

changes.summary: Enterprise base model no longer contains subscription fields
changes.description: The core Enterprise object returned by GET /enterprises, GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId, and related list endpoints no longer includes subscription-related fields (subscriptionPlanId, subscriptionStatus, currentPeriodStart, currentPeriodEnd). These fields have been removed from the database and are now exclusively owned by the payment-service. If you need subscription state alongside enterprise data, use GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId/status or /summary which include a subscription_snapshot field.

The response body of GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId/status now includes an optional subscription_snapshot object. This field is populated in real-time from the payment-service. It will be null if the enterprise has no active subscription or if the payment-service is temporarily unreachable.

changes.summary: EnterpriseSummary response shape extended with subscription_snapshot
changes.description: The response body of GET /enterprises/:enterpriseId/summary now includes an optional subscription_snapshot object with the same structure as in the status endpoint.
